name: SHA-256 ACK CRC Computation
description: Tests MeshCore ACK CRC computation: SHA-256(timestamp_le || txt_type_attempt || text || sender_pub_key)[0..3]. Each vector presents the full ACK packet that would be returned, along with the crypto_context showing the plaintext fed to SHA-256. Vectors ack-crc-001..004 share timestamp/text/pub_key and differ only in the attempt nibble of txt_type_attempt, demonstrating that each retry produces a distinct ACK CRC.

vectors.description: ACK CRC for TXT_MSG 'hello', ts=0x12345678, attempt=0, zero pubkey
vectors.notes: SHA-256(timestamp(4) || txt_type_attempt(1) || text || sender_pub_key(32))[0..3] = 9E0CECB2 (LE). Displayed ack_crc field is the uint32 value 0xB2EC0C9E.

vectors.description: Same message as ack-crc-001 but attempt=1 — distinct CRC
vectors.notes: txt_type_attempt=0x01 (TXT_TYPE_PLAIN<<2 | attempt=1). Confirms attempt increment changes ACK CRC.

vectors.description: Same message as ack-crc-001 but attempt=2 — distinct CRC
Same message as ack-crc-001 but attempt=3 — distinct CRC (max attempt)
vectors.notes: attempt=3 is the maximum value representable in the 2-bit attempt field.
